body_txt: Hard rock is a Sub-Genre of rock , developed in the late 1960s on both sides of the Atlantic. In the US it was popularized by bands like Steppenwolf and Iron Butterfly , while in Britain it grew out of the late '60s Blues . Generally, it's rooted in Blues Rock and Psychedelic Rock . Its main definition is that it is considerably "harder" than conventional rock music, which means that guitars have an edgier snarl of distortion and the drumming is louder and more emphatic. Hard rock is characterized by a heavy use of distorted electric guitars played through big speaker stacks, bass guitar, drums, pianos and other keyboards (especially Hammond organ, but also synthesizers in the '80s and beyond), and powerful vocals, making it near-indistinguishable from some of the lighter Heavy Metal bands. Indeed, a lot of debates continue to be had regarding exactly where the dividing line is between hard rock and heavy metal. (The line can be a difficult one to draw, since a band considered to be "shockingly hard" in the '70s is going to sound quite normal [if not downright subdued] in the 2020s, the era of extreme metal.) That's the only definition you need, really. Hard rock doesn't have as much of a "scene" or subculture like Heavy Metal , though it was most popular back in The '70s . Nowadays, hard rock is rarer, having been effectively replaced officially by a few similar genres . Still, there are some acts, more "retro" in focus, which could still be classified as hard rock, such as Wolfmother and The Darkness . The term "hard rock" persists both as a catch-all term for hard-hitting music that isn't full-fledged metal, and as an umbrella term for all hard-edged rock music from Death Metal to Hardcore Punk to Grunge , so it still comes up a lot. "Butt rock", meanwhile, is one less-than-endearing term which is often lobbed at this type of music by its detractors. (This term supposedly alludes to radio stations that claimed to play "nothing but rock".) Contrast with Soft Rock , rock music that goes in the opposite direction of Hard rock.
